title: Leucanthemum heterophyllum
taxonomy: Leucanthemum heterophyllum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Leucanthemum, which includes various daisy-like plants. Within the genus, Leucanthemum heterophyllum is often grouped with other heterophyllous species. The specific classification of Leucanthemum heterophyllum can vary depending on the source and regional classification systems. This plant's classification is a part of ongoing scientific research and updates in botanical taxonomy.
uses: Leucanthemum heterophyllum has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Europe, for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are used to make teas and infusions that are believed to have a soothing effect on digestive issues and respiratory problems. In some traditional remedies, Leucanthemum heterophyllum is used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its antibacterial properties. Additionally, the plant's astringent properties are thought to help in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Leucanthemum heterophyllum for medicinal purposes.

intro: Leucanthemum heterophyllum, commonly known as the variable daisy, is a perennial plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula. The variable daisy is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum heterophyllum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
